title: Exercises: History of NIST PQC Initiative

exercises.title: Timeline Construction
exercises.description: Create a detailed timeline of the NIST PQC standardization process, marking all major milestones.
tasks: List all major dates from 2016 to 2024
Include the number of algorithms at each round
Note key events like the SIKE break
Identify when each FIPS standard was published
exercises.expectedOutput: A comprehensive timeline document with at least 10 key milestones and their significance
hints: Start with December 2016 call for proposals
Round 2 was January 2019, Round 3 was July 2020
Don't forget the draft standards in 2023

exercises.title: Cryptographic Family Comparison
exercises.description: Compare and contrast the five main post-quantum cryptographic families that were represented in the NIST competition.
tasks: Describe the mathematical foundation of each family
List the advantages and disadvantages of each
Identify which schemes from each family advanced to later rounds
Explain why lattice-based schemes dominated the final selections
exercises.expectedOutput: A comparison table with analysis of lattice-based, hash-based, code-based, multivariate, and isogeny-based cryptography
hints: Consider security assumptions, key sizes, and performance
Note that multivariate and isogeny-based schemes had significant breaks

exercises.title: SIKE Break Case Study
exercises.description: Analyze the SIKE break and its implications for post-quantum cryptography deployment.
tasks: Research how SIKE worked before it was broken
Explain why the attack was surprising (classical, not quantum)
Discuss what this teaches about cryptographic assumptions
Propose strategies to protect against similar surprises in deployed systems
exercises.expectedOutput: A case study document (500+ words) analyzing the SIKE break and its lessons
hints: The attack used mathematics from the 1990s applied in a new way
Consider the importance of algorithm diversity and hybrid approaches

exercises.title: NIST Competition Model Analysis
exercises.description: Compare the NIST PQC competition to previous NIST cryptographic competitions.
tasks: Research the AES competition (1997-2001)
Research the SHA-3 competition (2007-2012)
Compare timelines, number of submissions, and evaluation criteria
Explain why NIST uses open competitions for standardization
exercises.expectedOutput: A comparison document showing similarities and differences between NIST competitions
hints: AES had 15 submissions, SHA-3 had 64, PQC had 82
Consider the benefits of public cryptanalysis

exercises.title: Quantum Threat Assessment
exercises.description: Create a threat assessment document explaining the quantum computing threat to current cryptographic systems.
tasks: Explain Shor's algorithm at a high level
List all cryptographic primitives affected
Estimate the timeline for quantum computers capable of breaking RSA-2048
Recommend immediate actions for organizations
exercises.expectedOutput: A threat assessment briefing suitable for non-technical executives
hints: Focus on practical implications, not mathematical details
Consider the 'harvest now, decrypt later' threat
